title: Appétit App Store
description: Schema for apps.json — the data file powering an Appétit app store.

developer.description: Default developer name shown on app detail pages.

featured.description: Apps to feature in the carousel banner on the Discover page.

id.description: Must match an app id in the apps array.

headline.description: Small label above the title (e.g. 'NEW APP').

id.description: Category identifier used in app category arrays.

apps.description: The list of apps in the store.

iconStyle.description: Per-app icon display settings to handle different icon formats.

scale.description: CSS transform scale (e.g. 1.3 to zoom in on padded macOS icons).

objectFit.description: CSS object-fit for the icon image.

borderRadius.description: CSS border-radius for the icon container (e.g. '22%').

bgColor.description: Background color for the icon container (e.g. '#000000').

padding.description: CSS padding inside the icon container (e.g. '18%').

id.description: Unique app identifier. Used in URLs and deduplication.

developer.description: Override the store-level developer name for this app.

subtitle.description: Short tagline shown in list rows.

description.description: One-liner for list views and search.

longDescription.description: Full description for the detail page.

icon.description: URL to the app icon image (PNG or SVG).

iconEmoji.description: Fallback emoji when icon is null or fails to load.

category.description: Category IDs this app belongs to (e.g. ['macos', 'productivity']).

brew.description: Homebrew install command. Triggers the install modal.

installCommand.description: Alternative install command (e.g. 'npx my-app'). Triggers the install modal.

downloadUrl.description: Direct download URL (e.g. GitHub Releases latest). Shown as secondary button in install modal.

buyUrl.description: Purchase URL for paid apps. When price is not 'Free', a Buy button links here. If omitted, falls back to homepage or github.

features.description: Feature list shown on the detail page.

screenshots.description: Screenshot URLs shown in the detail page preview section.
